children.heading: Authority and limitations of Secretary; applicability
children.label: a Authority and limitations of Secretary; applicability
A
children.text: the Secretary may not establish, under section 321(n), 341, or 343 of this title, maximum limits on the potency of any synthetic or natural vitamin or mineral within a food to which this section applies;

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary may not classify any natural or synthetic vitamin or mineral (or combination thereof) as a drug solely because it exceeds the level of potency which the Secretary determines is nutritionally rational or useful;

Paragraph (1) shall not apply in the case of a vitamin, mineral, other ingredient of food, or food, which is represented for use by individuals in the treatment or management of specific diseases or disorders, by children, or by pregnant or lactating women. For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “children” means individuals who are under the age of twelve years.1So in original. Probably should be “paragraph”.1

children.heading: Labeling and advertising requirements for foods
children.label: b Labeling and advertising requirements for foods

children.text: A food to which this section applies shall not be deemed under to be misbranded solely because its label bears, in accordance with , all the ingredients in the food or its advertising contains references to ingredients in the food which are not vitamins or minerals.section 343 of this titlesection 343(i)(2) of this title

The labeling for any food to which this section applies may not list its ingredients which are not dietary supplement ingredients described in (i) except as a part of a list of all the ingredients of such food, and (ii) unless such ingredients are listed in accordance with applicable regulations under . To the extent that compliance with clause (i) of this subparagraph is impracticable or results in deception or unfair competition, exemptions shall be established by regulations promulgated by the Secretary.section 321(ff) of this titlesection 343 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: which is or contains any natural or synthetic vitamin or mineral, and

children.label: i
children.text: is intended for ingestion in tablet, capsule, powder, softgel, gelcap, or liquid form, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if not intended for ingestion in such a form, is not represented as conventional food and is not represented for use as a sole item of a meal or of the diet.

For purposes of paragraph (1)(B)(i), a food shall be considered as intended for ingestion in liquid form only if it is formulated in a fluid carrier and it is intended for ingestion in daily quantities measured in drops or similar small units of measure.

children.label: A
children.text: Supplying a special dietary need that exists by reason of a physical, physiological, pathological, or other condition, including but not limited to the condition of disease, convalescence, pregnancy, lactation, infancy, allergic hypersensitivity to food, underweight, overweight, or the need to control the intake of sodium.

children.label: B
children.text: Supplying a vitamin, mineral, or other ingredient for use by man to supplement his diet by increasing the total dietary intake.

children.label: C
children.text: Supplying a special dietary need by reason of being a food for use as the sole item of the diet.
